name: Druidic
description: You know the Druidic language and can speak with plants and animals. You can use this language to communicate with plants and animals as if they were a human.

name: Timeless Body
description: For every 10 years that pass, your body only ages by 1 year.

name: Beast Spells
description: You can cast spells while Wild Shaped. You can perform the somatic and verbal components of spells, but you aren't able to provide material components.

name: Archdruid
description: You can use your Wild Shape an unlimited number of times.

name: Natural Recovery
description: During a short rest, you choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that less than or equal to half of your druid level rounded up, and none of the slots can be 6th level or higher. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

name: Land's Stride
description: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them, and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. You have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement, such as those created by the Entangle spell.

name: Nature's Ward
description: You can't be charmed or frightened by elementals or fey, and you are immune to poison and disease.

name: Nature's Sanctuary
description: When a beast or plant creature attacks you, that creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your druid spell save DC. On a failed save, taht creature must choose a different target, or the attack automatically misses. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours. The creature is aware of this effect before it makes its attack against you.

name: Primal Strike
description: Your attacks in Beast form count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

name: Hearth of Moonlight and Shadow
description: At the beginning of a Short or Long Rest, you can create an invisible 30-foot radius sphere. While within the sphere, you and your allies gain a +5 bonus to Dsxterity (Stealth) and Wisdom (Perception) checks, and light produced within the sphere is not visible from outside it.

name: Walker in Dreams
description: When you finish a short rest, you can cast Dream, Scrying, or Teleportation Circle without expending a spell slot. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. If you cast Teleportation Circle this way, it takes you to the last location where you finished a Long Rest on the current plane.

name: Speech of the Woods
description: You can speak, read, and write Sylvan. Beasts can understand your speech, and you can decipher their noises and motions. They cannot convey or understand sophisticated concepts, but they can communicate basic information.

name: Spirit Totem: Bear Spirit
description: Each creature of your choice in the aura when the spirit appears gains temporary hit points equal to 5 + your druid level. You and your allies gain Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws made while in the aura.

name: Spirit Totem: Hawk Spirit
description: You and your allies have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks while in the aura.

name: Spirit Totem: Unicorn Spirit
description: You and your allies gain advantage on all ability checks made to detect creatures in the spirit's aura. If you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points to any creature inside or outside the aura, each creature of your choice in the aura also regains hit points equal to your druid level.

name: Mighty Summoner
description: Any beasts or fey summoned or created by a spell that you cast gains the following benefits. It gains an extra 2 Hit Points per Hit Die it has. The damage from its natural weapons is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity or resistance to nonmagical attacks and damage.

name: Guardian Spirit
description: When a beast or fey that you summoned or created with a spell ends its turn in your Spirit Totem aura, that creature regains a number of hit points equal to half your druid level.
